# Title

Relationship Between Average Token Entropy (Bits/Token) and Character Error Rate (CER)

## Purpose of the Figure

This figure evaluates whether page length affects the relationship between average token entropy (bits/token) and character error rate (CER). The figure indicates that entropy increases as page length increases, and CER tends to increase as well. Entropy generally remains lower for shorter excerpts, with a small number of outliers.

## Generated By

[stratified_analysis.py](../../scripts/stratified_analysis.py)

## Input Files Used

[results_k_10.csv](../../results/csv/results_k_10.csv)

## X-axis

Average Token Surprisal (Bits/Token)

## Y-axis

Average Token Entropy (Bits/Token)

## Units

| Field | Units |
|---|---|
| Average Token Surprisal | Bits/Token |
| Average Token Entropy | Bits/Token |

## Other Information

Abbreviations:

| Label | Description |
|---|---|
| Q1 | Quartile 1 (shortest ground-truth length; 25 samples) |
| Q2 | Quartile 2 (next shortest ground-truth length; 25 samples) |
| Q3 | Quartile 3 (next shortest ground-truth length; 25 samples) |
| Q4 | Quartile 4 (longest ground-truth length; 25 samples) |

- Sample size: the plot consists of 100 randomized page excerpts.
- Filtering rules: samples with a CER > 2% were filtered from the dataset.   
- Normalization choices: whitespace was normalized, punctuation was removed entirely, and all characters were converted to lowercase for both ground-truth and OCR texts.

## Figure Importance

This figure shows that page length has a considerable effect on average token entropy, which correlates with CER. This suggests that page length affects OCR error rates.
